executive_summary: The ARC is a small, expertise-heavy non-corporate Commonwealth entity that administers the ~$1bn/year National Competitive Grants Program (NCGP) and provides research policy advice. FY2025-26 is a once-in-a-generation reform year: the Board is redesigning the entire NCGP off the back of its Policy Review, building a new 10-year ARC Strategy, and developing a new data-driven National Research Evaluation and Impact Framework in response to the Universities Accord. This reform agenda touches every stage of the funding cycle (assessment, post-award, evaluation) and is underpinned by data/analytics uplift, the RMS platform, a new information management system, cyber and privacy improvements, and an office relocation. The agency also operates under live external pressure — recurring ministerial-veto/research-security controversy, ANAO interest in grants administration, and the new governance regime following the ARC Act amendments — making independent assurance, program delivery and data work credible (if modestly sized) BD prospects. Note the plan was supplied as the 2025-26 plan despite a 2024-25 source URL.

strategic_priorities.title: Redesign and implement a revitalised NCGP
strategic_priorities.description: Provide government with recommendations for a simpler NCGP and then transition to a new model from 2026, rethinking the assessment model, post-award grant management and evaluation across the whole funding cycle.
strategic_priorities.source_quote: The bold reforms will require rethinking all stages of the funding cycle including the assessment model, post-award grant management and evaluation.

strategic_priorities.title: Deliver a 10-year ARC Strategy
strategic_priorities.description: Develop a long-term strategy to guide the ~$1bn annual NCGP investment, align with national priorities and provide stewardship for a diverse, thriving research sector.
strategic_priorities.source_quote: we will develop a 10-year ARC Strategy to guide the significant $1 billion annual investment made in early stage research through the NCGP.

strategic_priorities.title: Build a National Research Evaluation and Impact Framework
strategic_priorities.description: Develop a modern, data-driven framework to assess the excellence, impact and depth of university research, responding to the Universities Accord recommendation for intelligent-technology-based evaluation; FY2025-26 deliverable is a proposal.
strategic_priorities.source_quote: the ARC is working to move to a modern, data-driven National Research Evaluation and Impact Framework.

strategic_priorities.title: Strengthen research integrity and security
strategic_priorities.description: Continue work with the Department of Education, NHMRC and national security agencies to safeguard research integrity and protect Australian research from foreign interference risks.
strategic_priorities.source_quote: Continuing to work with the Department of Education, national security agencies and other stakeholders to further strengthen processes to protect the security of Australian research from foreign interference risks.

strategic_priorities.title: Develop enhanced data, analytics and communication capabilities
strategic_priorities.description: Build data/analytics capability to better understand the research landscape, evaluate NCGP performance and tell the story of ARC-funded research; uplift data literacy per the ARC Data Strategy.
strategic_priorities.source_quote: Developing enhanced data, analytics and communication capabilities that offer greater insight into the Australian research landscape.

strategic_priorities.title: Modernise technology and corporate infrastructure
strategic_priorities.description: Maintain and adapt RMS, implement a new information management system, optimise the website, improve cyber security, uplift privacy maturity, and relocate to new premises.
strategic_priorities.source_quote: Implement a new information management system to improve the management of the ARC's records and information holdings.

reform_and_change_drivers: NCGP Policy Review final report due to government Q3 2025, with progressive transition to a new funding model from 2026
Australian Universities Accord Final Report response driving a new, data-driven research evaluation approach
Revised Australian Research Council Act 2001 and new ARC Board/governance arrangements (post ARC Act amendment legislation)
Establishment of the Australian Tertiary Education Commission and the Strategic Examination of Research and Development reshaping the sector
Heightened foreign-interference and research-security risk environment
Growth of AI creating new opportunities and risks for ARC operations and the research sector
APS Strategic Commissioning Framework pressure to reduce outsourcing of core program/project management work
challenges_and_risks: Small agency with 'big ambition' delivering a major multi-year reform with constrained resources, requiring reprioritisation
Reported job cuts (Dec 2025) potentially reducing internal delivery capacity at the height of reform
Strategic risk of failing to provide strong leadership on research strategy
Strategic risk that ARC fails to comply with legislative requirements and key government policies
Strategic risk of not engaging effectively with diverse stakeholders
Legacy/critical dependence on the RMS platform that must be adapted to support a redesigned NCGP
Privacy maturity and cyber security explicitly flagged as needing uplift
APS Strategic Commissioning Framework limits reliance on contractors for core program/project management — a constraint on certain engagement types

news_and_controversies.summary: Ministerial veto of six peer-reviewed ARC grants on 'national interest' grounds (Dec 2021) triggered sustained sector backlash and College of Experts resignations, with fallout continuing into late 2024.
news_and_controversies.implication: Grant-integrity, governance and independent process-assurance work is a live demand area; lead with independence and probity credibility.

news_and_controversies.summary: In 2025 a Labor minister again declined/vetoed ARC grants on national-security grounds, reportedly causing confusion and delays despite reform; ARC issued an 'Update on Research Security'.
news_and_controversies.implication: National-interest/security screening of research is an active operational pain point — scope for due-diligence and process-design advisory around research security frameworks.
news_and_controversies.source: Times Higher Education; Research Professional News; ARC — https://www.arc.gov.au/news-and-publications/media/update-research-security

news_and_controversies.summary: Australian Research Council job cuts reported on 4 December 2025.
news_and_controversies.implication: Workforce reduction during a major reform may increase reliance on external delivery and assurance support, or signal tighter external spend — clarify early in any approach.

anao_findings.summary: ANAO performance audit on the ARC's administration of the National Competitive Grants Program (flagship grants program); specific findings not confirmed in research and date may predate the 3-year window.
anao_findings.implication: Standing ANAO interest in grants administration points to recurring assurance, controls and process-improvement needs as the NCGP is redesigned.

anao_findings.summary: ANAO performance audit on the ARC's management of research grants (grant lifecycle); findings/date to be verified.
anao_findings.implication: Grant lifecycle management is an audited risk area — opportunity for grant-management framework and compliance support during transition to a new model.

anao_findings.summary: ANAO audit requests on ARC's administration of funding application processes and administration of grants (listed Sept 2021).
anao_findings.implication: Funding-application process integrity has been flagged to ANAO; process-design and assurance work is relevant as assessment models are reformed.

parliamentary_matters.summary: The Senate Education and Employment Legislation Committee examined the Australian Research Council Amendment (Review Response) Bill 2023, responding to the independent ARC Act review and reforming the ministerial grant-approval (veto) power.
parliamentary_matters.implication: A recent legislative/governance reset means new operating frameworks, transition arrangements and implementation support — a timely entry point for governance and program advisory.

parliamentary_matters.summary: Government framed the reforms as 'Ending the days of political interference in the Australian Research Council' (Minister Clare, March 2024), tying the legislative process to the veto controversy.
parliamentary_matters.implication: Reform is a ministerial priority with political ownership; engagement should align to the stated independence/governance agenda.

opportunity_areas.agency_need: Implement a new NCGP model from 2026 across assessment, post-award management and evaluation — a large, multi-year change for a small agency facing job cuts.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: A program/PMO setup and embedded delivery support for the NCGP transition, with a benefits-management framework and staged delivery planning — sized to ARC's capacity and mindful of the APS Strategic Commissioning Framework's limits on core PM outsourcing.
opportunity_areas.rationale: The plan explicitly names a long-term reform requiring rethinking of all funding-cycle stages, with progressive rollout from 2026; recurring ANAO interest in grants administration strengthens the case for disciplined delivery.

opportunity_areas.area: Independent assurance of the NCGP reform and grants administration
opportunity_areas.agency_need: Maintain integrity and compliance of grants administration through a high-profile reform amid veto controversy and ANAO scrutiny.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: Independent gateway-style assurance / health checks of the NCGP redesign at key stages, plus a grants-administration controls review aligned to the Commonwealth Grant Rules and Principles.
opportunity_areas.rationale: The plan commits to reviewing grant administration to maintain compliance; ANAO has repeatedly examined ARC grants administration and the veto issue keeps integrity in the spotlight.

opportunity_areas.area: National Research Evaluation and Impact Framework design
opportunity_areas.agency_need: Develop a new data-driven framework to assess university research excellence, impact and depth, replacing ERA per the Universities Accord.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: A design engagement for the evaluation framework — methodology, performance logic and consultation support — delivered with a small senior team, complemented by data expertise for the evidence base.
opportunity_areas.rationale: Performance measure 10 commits the ARC to produce a proposal for a new framework in 2025-26, responding directly to the Accord recommendation for intelligent-technology-based evaluation.

opportunity_areas.agency_need: Harvest and analyse research data, evaluate NCGP performance, and improve accessibility/reliability of ARC data holdings per the ARC Data Strategy.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: An information/data strategy and analytics uplift — implementing tooling to harvest and analyse research-system data, build business intelligence on NCGP performance, and improve data literacy and holdings management.
opportunity_areas.rationale: The plan names enhanced data/analytics as a key priority and details data, harvesting and data-literacy commitments under Capabilities.

opportunity_areas.area: RMS adaptation and new information management system
opportunity_areas.agency_need: Adapt the core RMS platform to support a redesigned NCGP and implement a new information/records management system.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: Business and process analysis, service design and solutions implementation to evolve RMS for new schemes and to stand up the new information management system, including integration and transition support.
opportunity_areas.rationale: Technology priorities explicitly include maintaining/adapting RMS and implementing a new information management system; both are directly tied to the NCGP reform.

opportunity_areas.agency_need: Strengthen processes to protect Australian research from foreign-interference risk, working with national security agencies.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: A research-security framework and process-design review aligning ARC grant processes with whole-of-government foreign-interference frameworks; secondary scope for assurance of the controls.
opportunity_areas.rationale: Named as a key priority and reinforced by 2025 national-security veto controversy and ARC's own research-security update; note clearance/access constraints when scoping.

opportunity_areas.agency_need: Review and update ARC performance frameworks to align with new strategic and policy directions and new evaluation methodologies.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: A PGPA-aligned performance-measure redesign linking the new 10-year Strategy and NCGP objectives to a coherent measurement framework.
opportunity_areas.rationale: The plan commits to reviewing/updating performance frameworks and establishing new NCGP evaluation methodologies; several measures were newly adjusted this year.

opportunity_areas.area: Cyber security and privacy maturity uplift
opportunity_areas.agency_need: Improve cyber security to protect ARC and stakeholder data, and uplift privacy maturity, policies and procedures.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: A security assessment of ARC systems (notably RMS) and a privacy-maturity uplift advisory, producing a prioritised remediation roadmap.
opportunity_areas.rationale: Technology and process priorities explicitly commit to improving cyber security measures and uplifting the agency's privacy maturity rating.

opportunity_areas.agency_need: Deliver a new ARC Communications and Engagement Plan and rebuild stakeholder trust across universities, researchers and government.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: Stakeholder engagement strategy and communications support for the NCGP redesign and evaluation-framework consultations, including content to 'tell the story' of ARC-funded research.
opportunity_areas.rationale: The plan names a new Communications and Engagement Plan and identifies ineffective stakeholder engagement as a strategic risk; trust has been strained by the veto controversy.

recommended_actions.action: Approach the Deputy CEO and the reform/program leadership owning the NCGP redesign with a tailored capability statement on program delivery plus independent assurance, sized to a small agency and respectful of APS Strategic Commissioning Framework limits.
recommended_actions.rationale: The NCGP redesign and 2026 transition is the largest, most time-bound demand in the plan and the clearest fit for delivery and assurance work.

recommended_actions.action: Prepare a short thought piece on data-driven research evaluation frameworks and offer a scoping conversation with the team developing the National Research Evaluation and Impact Framework, given the 2025-26 proposal deliverable.
recommended_actions.rationale: Performance measure 10 creates a concrete, dated deliverable that maps cleanly to Strategy, Policy & Governance and Data, Analytics & Information.

recommended_actions.action: Engage the Chief Risk Officer function / ARC Audit and Risk Committee secretariat with a grants-administration assurance and controls offering, referencing ANAO's recurring interest in NCGP administration.
recommended_actions.rationale: Reform plus ANAO and parliamentary scrutiny make independent assurance an easy-to-justify, lower-political-risk engagement.

recommended_actions.action: Monitor for procurements linked to RMS adaptation, the new information management system, and data/analytics tooling as the NCGP model is finalised after the Q3 2025 report to government.
recommended_actions.rationale: These ICT/data builds will be triggered by the reform timetable and represent the firm's ability to build and implement, not just advise.

recommended_actions.action: Clarify the impact of the December 2025 job cuts on external delivery appetite early in any engagement.
recommended_actions.rationale: Workforce reductions during peak reform can either expand or contract external spend; understanding this shapes which engagement shapes are viable.

engagement_targets: Deputy CEO (oversees grant policy/administration, program design and delivery, and reform)
NCGP reform / Program Implementation Committee leadership
Director, Governance and Corporate Reporting (corporate plan contact, governance/assurance entry point)
ARC Audit and Risk Committee and Chief Risk Officer function
CIO / technology function owning RMS and the new information management system
Academic Directors and the team developing the National Research Evaluation and Impact Framework
Communications and Engagement function (new Communications and Engagement Plan)
talking_points: The NCGP redesign touches every stage of the funding cycle and rolls out from 2026 — how is delivery being resourced, and where would independent program assurance add the most value given ANAO's recurring interest in grants administration?

With a 2025-26 proposal due for the National Research Evaluation and Impact Framework, we can bring evaluation methodology and data-driven design support that meets the Accord's intelligent-technology expectations.
As a small agency facing reported job cuts while delivering once-in-a-generation reform, right-sized embedded delivery and assurance can de-risk the transition without overbuilding.
Your data and analytics ambitions — harvesting research-system data and uplifting holdings and literacy — map directly to an information and BI capability we can both design and build.
Research security and national-interest screening remain operationally contested; we can help design defensible, whole-of-government-aligned processes (non-legal).
RMS adaptation and the new information management system are reform-critical builds where we can deliver discovery through implementation, not just advice.
caveats: The plan is clear and reform-rich, giving high confidence on the strategic direction; however, the ARC is a small agency with modest budget, so engagements must be right-sized (short advisory, framework design, targeted delivery/assurance) rather than large transformation programs. The APS Strategic Commissioning Framework explicitly constrains outsourcing of core program/project management work, which limits some Programs, Projects & Change scope. The supplied document is the FY2025-26 plan despite a 2024-25 source URL. External research relied on headline/source-level metadata; several ANAO audits may predate the 3-year window and their specific findings were not confirmed — verify before client use. Cyber, privacy and ICT opportunities are real but lightly described, hence Medium confidence.
